description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring two large, rectangular solar panel arrays mounted on a low, flat structure. The panels are dark, likely made of photovoltaic cells, and are set at a slight angle to optimize sunlight capture. The base appears to be constructed from concrete or metal, providing stability and support for the panels. There are small, box-like components on the base, possibly housing electrical equipment or junction boxes. The overall design is compact and functional, suitable for placement on rooftops or open ground in urban environments. The structure is robust, with minimal visible cabling and a clean, industrial appearance.
description: A compact solar energy installation with two angled dark solar panels on a sturdy, low-profile base, featuring small equipment housings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the two large, angled solar panels and the low, rectangular base with visible equipment housings.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt of the solar panels and the depth of the supporting base structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the solar panels and additional equipment housings on the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the panel tilt and the arrangement of the base components.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly discernible, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a low-profile metal and concrete support structure
attribute_array_configuration: two-panel linear row, panels mounted side by side on a single base
